body.title: CC shim: cc_mode setting (CC.0 only / CC.0+CC.1 / max=n)
body.result: cc_concurrency_mode in cc.yaml drives Granny max-concurrent; cc0_only blocks second dispatch; 8 new tests
body.description:
CC concurrency is currently hardcoded as CC.0-only via granny.yaml max_concurrent: 1.

Design: move the concurrency mode into CC shim config (not Granny config — the shim knows its own session identity). Three modes:
  [ ] CC.0 only — this session is the sole CC; no background workers
  [ ] CC.0 + CC.1 — one interactive (CC.0) plus one background (CC.1)
  [ ] max=n — up to n concurrent CC sessions

Current setting: CC.0 only (context load is noticeably heavier with multiple sessions).

Rationale: context load overhead is real and measurable — running CC.0 + CC.1 for background work costs ~25% more context per turn. CC.0-only is the right default until a specific use case justifies the cost.

Setting lives in: config/profiles/cc.yaml under cc_concurrency_mode.
Granny reads this when deciding whether to offer a ticket to a background CC session.

**Affected files:** config/profiles/cc.yaml (add cc_concurrency_mode field), devices/granny/daemon.py (read setting before cc0 dispatch), granny.yaml (remove max_concurrent: 1 — now derived from cc profile)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; shim config is canonical source for worker identity settings
**Scope boundary:** IN: cc_concurrency_mode field + Granny reads it. OUT: actual CC.1 session management (that is T-granny-cc1-dispatch, follow-on)
**Completion criteria:** granny.yaml no longer hardcodes max_concurrent; cc.yaml has cc_concurrency_mode: cc0_only; Granny respects it; test covers cc0_only blocks a second dispatch
